intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data confirms the presence of two primary containers. The yellow pot (P1) contains a soil-based substrate with a visible sensor tag. The black pot (P3/P4) contains a white rabbit anchor and organic debris. The registry-expected P2 (Mexican Mint) is not visually identifiable as a living specimen.

plant_audit.P1: Soil surface appears dark and potentially saturated. No visible String of Nickels foliage.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor is present. Substrate contains light-colored granular debris (likely perlite or eggshell fragments). No healthy Pothos foliage detected.
plant_audit.P4: No evidence of Silver Guest; pot appears to be the same vessel as P3.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, suggesting high moisture content or peat-heavy mix.
biome_observations.debris: White granular material (likely perlite or crushed eggshell) present in P3/P4.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, no significant organic matter outside of pots.

temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Return to baseline lighting; no new growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of dormancy or failure. There is a total absence of visible chlorophyll-active foliage across all pots. The presence of a sensor in P1 suggests an experimental or monitoring phase, but the lack of plant matter indicates a failed or non-started cultivation cycle.
anomalies: New Introduction: Sensor tag in P1.
New Introduction: White granular debris in P3/P4.
Systemic Loss: All expected plant specimens (P1-P4) are currently invisible or deceased.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a static, non-vegetated environment. The pots contain substrate and monitoring equipment, but no living plant matter is discernable. The 'white rabbit' anchor in P3 is the only consistent landmark. The lack of growth over the 5-day observation window suggests either a dormant state, a recent clearing of the pots, or a failure to germinate/thrive.
